PARAM.description: the tree must be a BSP tree
PARAM.assertion: assert receiverObjectID instanceof org.openjena.arq.model.BSPTree && args[0] != null;
PARAM.name: newTree
PARAM.content:
This method allow to create new instances without knowing

exactly the type of the region. It is an application of the

prototype design pattern.

The leaf nodes of the BSP tree must have a

{@code Boolean} attribute representing the inside status of

the corresponding cell (true for inside cells, false for outside cells).

RETURN.description: A new region, copy of the instance.
RETURN.content: A new region, copy of the instance.

RETURN.description: true if the instance is empty.
RETURN.content: true if the instance is empty.

PARAM.description: the given node must have Region tree semantics
PARAM.name: node
PARAM.content: must have Boolean attributes representing an inside/outside property

RETURN.description: true if the sub-tree starting at the given node is empty
RETURN.content: true if the sub-tree starting at the given node is empty

RETURN.description: true if the instance covers the full space.
RETURN.content: true if the instance covers the full space.

PARAM.description: the root node must have Region tree semantics
PARAM.name: node
PARAM.content: <em>must</em> have {@link Region Region} tree semantics, i.e. the leaf nodes must have {@code Boolean} attributes representing an inside/outside property

RETURN.name: true
RETURN.content: if the sub-tree starting at the given node covers the full space
RETURN.description: if the sub-tree starting at the given node covers the full space

PARAM.name: region
PARAM.content: region to check against the instance
PARAM.description: region to check against the instance

RETURN.description: true if the instance contains the specified region.
RETURN.content: true if the instance contains the specified region.

PARAM.assertion: assert receiverObjectID != null && receiverObjectID instanceof Location;
PARAM.name: point
PARAM.content: The point to check must be valid.

RETURN.name: a
RETURN.content: code representing the point status: either {@link Location#INSIDE}, {@link Location#OUTSIDE} or {@link Location#BOUNDARY}
RETURN.description: code representing the point status: either {@link Location#INSIDE}, {@link Location#OUTSIDE} or {@link Location#BOUNDARY}

PARAM.description: the point must be of type Point
PARAM.name: point
PARAM.content: the point must be of type Point

RETURN.description: projection of the point on the boundary
RETURN.content: projection of the point on the boundary

PARAM.description: includeBoundaryAttributes must be of type boolean
PARAM.name: includeBoundaryAttributes

RETURN.description: the size of the boundary (this is 0 in 1D, a length in 2D, an area in 3D ...).
RETURN.content: the size of the boundary (this is 0 in 1D, a length in 2D, an area in 3D ...).

RETURN.description: the size of the instance (this is a length in 1D, an area in 2D, a volume in 3D ...).
RETURN.content: the size of the instance (this is a length in 1D, an area in 2D, a volume in 3D ...)

RETURN.description: <code>Compute the relative position of the instance with respect to an hyperplane.</code>
RETURN.assertion: assert methodResultID == (args[0] != null && args[0].equals(Side.HYPER) ? Side.HYPER : (receiverObjectID.getTree().contains(args[0]) || receiverObjectID.getTree().isEmpty() || receiverObjectID.isFull()) ? Side.BOTH : (receiverObjectID.getTree().isEmpty() || receiverObjectID.isFull()) ? Side.PLUS : Side.MINUS);
RETURN.content: <code>Compute the relative position of the instance with respect to an hyperplane.</code>

PARAM.description: The parts of the sub-hyperplane that belong to the boundary are not included in the resulting parts.
PARAM.name: sub
PARAM.content: The parts of the sub-hyperplane that belong to the boundary are not included in the resulting parts.

RETURN.description: Get the parts of a sub-hyperplane that are contained in the region.
RETURN.content: Get the parts of a sub-hyperplane that are contained in the region. The parts of the sub-hyperplane that belong to the boundary are not included in the resulting parts.
